behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real model judgment quality on legal text extraction. A stubbed extractor would always return expected keys and hide misses on implicit phrasing, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Rejected Workflow because behavior covers one reviewer only (not multiple agentic steps in sequence).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a labeled set of real contracts and score recall for parties/dates/obligations/penalties; fail if required recall threshold is not met or key-term category omissions appear.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: semantic risk judgment from a real AI reviewer. Indirect/euphemistic phrasing is exactly where deterministic mocks pass but real model quality fails. Rejected Workflow because this behavior is a single reviewer’s output quality, not a chained multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real risk-assessor model on a curated corpus containing direct and indirect/euphemistic risky clauses under a concrete risk policy; fail on low detection rate or missed euphemistic cases.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: correctness depends on real AI interpretation of contract text against jurisdictional requirements. A deterministic stub would not expose misclassification of nuanced compliance conditions. Rejected Workflow because only one reviewer is under test; no required multi-agent sequence in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance-checker model against jurisdiction-labeled contract scenarios (compliant/non-compliant) and assert decision accuracy and rationale alignment with the configured jurisdiction.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says "standard" but risk assessor says "high risk"), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: module handoff/wiring between reviewer outputs and merge logic (schema + attribution propagation). Integration is sufficient with deterministic fixture outputs from two reviewer modules. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed, so not System; (b) correctness does not depend on real AI output quality for this behavior, so not Agentic; (c) no chained agentic steps required, so not Workflow. Rejected Unit because failure commonly occurs at cross-module data contract boundaries ('two reviewers... conflicting assessments' and 'preserves both... with attribution').
behaviors.test_description: Wire real reviewer-output adapters (or equivalent production serializers) into the merge module, inject conflicting clause assessments, and assert unified report retains both assessments with correct reviewer attribution and no overwrite.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: multiple real agentic steps in sequence (three AI reviewers + merge orchestration). Failures can arise from parallel dispatch/collection plus agentic outputs and final aggregation, which lower-level tests can miss. Rejected Agentic as minimum because behavior explicitly requires full multi-step pipeline ('dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging').
behaviors.test_description: Execute the end-to-end pipeline on real contracts using all three real AI reviewers in parallel, then verify the final report contains every reviewer finding (no dropped findings) and correct merge output under concurrent completion ordering.
